title: 5 interior offensive linemen options for Patriots after Garrett Bradbury trade
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots to seek new left guard after trading center Garrett Bradbury to the Bears, reshuffling their offensive line.
Isaac Seumalo from the Steelers is a top free agent option, ranking high in pass and run block win rates.
Oregon's Emmanuel Pregnon stands out as a draft prospect, projected to be a Day 2 pick with elite performance metrics.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for the ‘amazing’ Make-A-Wish children
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jason Perry, 48, from Stockton, California, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Make-A-Wish Foundation.
Perry is motivated by the resilience and hope of Wish children in the Greater Massachusetts-Rhode Island region.
Over 15 years, Perry has been involved in granting wishes, drawing inspiration from the children's courage and positivity.

title: Insider predicts Patriots sign Seahawks standout pass rusher, but don’t land Alec Pierce in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots are expected to sign Seahawks' edge rusher Boye Mafe with a projected three-year, $48 million contract.
Boye Mafe, 27, recorded 44 pressures and a 17.4% pass rush win rate in the 2025 season.
Patriots unlikely to sign Colts receiver Alec Pierce, projected to join Chargers with a three-year, $80 million deal.

title: Review & setlist: With sharp sarcasm, Jesse Welles took on war, bullies, and Trump at the House of Blues
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Jesse Welles performed at Citizens House of Blues Boston on March 6, part of his 'Under the Powerlines Tour.'
The setlist included politically charged songs like 'Sometimes You Bomb Iran' and covers like 'Paranoid' by Black Sabbath.
Welles's performance featured satirical and poignant commentary on sociopolitical issues, engaging a responsive crowd.

title: Masataka Yoshida hits a home run in Japan’s win vs. Korea, with Drake Maye in attendance
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Masataka Yoshida helped Japan win 8-6 over Korea with a home run and three RBIs in the World Baseball Classic.
Yoshida's third-inning homer was part of a streak alongside Shohei Ohtani and Seiya Suzuki, giving Japan a 5-3 lead.
Patriots quarterback Drake Maye attended the game in Tokyo, where Yoshida's performance moved Japan to 2-0 in pool play.

title: This Roslindale native is running her first Boston Marathon for Mass. Eye and Ear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Makayla Aboujaoude-Quirk, 25, from Roslindale, will run her first marathon in Boston for Mass. Eye and Ear.
Aboujaoude-Quirk has been a patient at Mass. Eye and Ear for nearly a decade, with eye conditions now managed.
Mass. Eye and Ear supports research in eye, ear, nose, and throat conditions and has a specialized 24-hour emergency department.

title: It was overdue, but Tim Legler was promoted to ESPN/ABC’s top NBA broadcast team this season, and he’s thriving
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Tim Legler has been promoted to ESPN/ABC's top NBA broadcast team, joining Mike Breen and Richard Jefferson.
Legler, a respected analyst since 2000, now calls games that include the NBA Finals, following promotions in August 2025.
The promotion follows instability as ESPN/ABC reshuffled teams, with Doc Rivers and JJ Redick leaving for coaching roles.

title: Cooper Flagg was the Maine story in Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics lineup after a 298-day recovery, scoring 15 points with 12 rebounds and 7 assists.
Cooper Flagg made his NBA debut at TD Garden, contributing 16 points, 8 rebounds, and 6 assists for the Dallas Mavericks.
Hundreds of fans from Maine attended Flagg's debut at TD Garden, showcasing a strong local connection and support.

title: Charles Barkley impressed by Jayson Tatum in return, but doesn’t view Celtics as favorites in East
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics lineup after 298 days, contributing 15 points, 12 rebounds, and seven assists.
Charles Barkley impressed by Tatum's return but maintains the Cavaliers as favorites to win the Eastern Conference.
Cleveland has an 8-1 record since acquiring James Harden, reinforcing Barkley's choice of them as his Eastern favorite.

title: Mass. man accused of impersonating an Army veteran for more than 30 years
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: James D. Sommers of Pittsfield charged with impersonating a U.S. Army veteran for over 30 years.
Sommers allegedly obtained thousands in Social Security and nearly $30,000 in VA medical care.
Sommers faces up to five years in prison if convicted of making false statements, prosecutors say.
